items.title: Defoe leaves Woking after four months in charge
items.summary: Former England and Tottenham striker Jermain Defoe has described his first managerial job as an "eye-opening experience" following his departure from National League side Woking after just four months and nine matches in charge.

items.title: Arsenal and Tottenham set to go head to head for £51m-rated World Cup star, 21, who faced England
items.summary: ARSENAL and Tottenham are preparing to go head-to-head in a fierce transfer battle for RB Leipzig winger Antonio Nusa. items.title: Jermain Defoe has contract terminated just four months after England icon landed first managerial job
items.summary: JERMAIN DEFOE says he is "really disappointed" after his first job in management ended after only SIX competitive matches. The Premier League legend, 43, has left Woking by mutual consent just four months on from his appointment. Defoe guided the non-league minnows to a tenth place finish in the Nat

items.title: Conference League clash SUSPENDED for extreme weather with players forced off pitch
items.summary: A EUROPA Conference League qualifier between Aluminij and Dinamo City was suspended after lightning and torrential rain forced players off the pitch in Slovenia. items.title: ‘Exhausted’ Erling Haaland and Wag don pyjama-style outfits as they party in millionaire’s playground Saint-Tropez
items.summary: ERLING Haaland and his Wag looked like good bedfellows in pyjama and nightie-style outfits as they partied in millionaire’s playground Saint-Tropez. items.title: Why Arsenal splashed £34m on former Norwich flop Christos Tzolis who models his game on World Cup winner
items.summary: ARSENAL new boy Christos Tzolis models his game  on legendary Spain forward David Villa. And the Gunners will  hope the Greek winger can add goals to their attack after splashing out £34million to sign him on a five-year deal from Club Brugge. items.title: From plucky minnows to England heroes, Golden Boot showdown and Viking rows… why the 2026 World Cup was greatest ever
items.summary: FIFA’S Team of Tournament features England’s Jude Bellingham, France’s Kylian Mbappe, Argentina’s Lionel Mess . . . and an unlikely hero in Cape Verde’s goalkeeper Vozinha.
